role: ArchSpine is a semantic mirror system that automatically analyzes, indexes, and enforces architecture for any codebase, producing structured knowledge for humans and AI agents.
responsibility: The system is organized into a multi-stage pipeline. Template definitions in src/assets standardize documentation and prompts. The AST layer (src/ast) parses source code into structured symbols and imports using language-specific rules (src/ast/rules). The CLI layer (src/cli) handles user commands, initialization, and repository setup. The core layer (src/core) orchestrates L2 aggregation from multiple L1 sources, coordinating data collection, validation, and state construction. Engines (src/engines) perform scanning, rule matching (from .spine/rules), context resolution, violation detection, and fix generation. Infrastructure modules (src/infra) provide configuration management, SQLite persistence with schema migration, LLM integration with multiple provider adapters, secure credential storage, an MCP server for AI agent interaction, manifest state persistence, and prompt construction utilities. Services (src/services) coordinate multi-stage pipelines for architectural validation, automated fixes, synchronization, view generation, and runtime management. Tasks (src/tasks) implement each pipeline stage with checkpointing, concurrency, and caching. Types (src/types) define core data contracts and interfaces for mirrored units, dependency graphs, and view artifacts. Utilities (src/utils) support file synchronization, path normalization, and locking. Tests (tests) cover unit, integration, and end-to-end scenarios across all layers, ensuring correctness and robustness.
